preview_text:
The model favors South Korea at 61%, with South Africa assigned a 13% chance of victory. The underdog faces a substantial probability gap, with the forecast pointing clearly toward a Korean advantage on a neutral pitch.

The win-draw-loss spread reflects a moderately competitive match shaped by Korea's edge. A draw at roughly 26% represents a plausible outcome, neither rare nor central to the expected result. The 48-point gap between Korea's win probability and South Africa's suggests Korea enters as clear favorite without the match being a foregone conclusion.

The model and sportsbook consensus are closely aligned across all three outcomes. The model places South Africa 4 percentage points lower than the market median, while Korea sits 3 points higher in the model's view. Polymarket prices track near-identically to the consensus. The minimal divergence across sources indicates broad agreement on match structure.
